'''Neil Fraser Cowie''' (born 16 January 1967) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] and [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]], and at club level for [[Rochdale Hornets]] and [[Wigan Warriors]], as a {{rlp|pr}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref>{{cite book| first1= Graham| last1= Williams| first2= Peter| last2= Lush| first3= David| last3= Farrar| title= The British Rugby League Records Book| publisher= London League| isbn= 978-1-903659-49-6| year= 2009| pages= 108–114}}</ref>

==Background==
Neil Cowie was born in [[Todmorden]], [[West Riding of Yorkshire]], [[England]] on 16 January 1967.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org" />

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Cowie signed for [[Rochdale Hornets]] in 1986 and went on to make 16 appearances in his first season. Over the next four seasons he became a regular in the Rochdale Hornets front row, occasionally playing as a {{rlp|sr}}. His blockbusting performances caught the attention of [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] and he signed for them at the end of the [[1990–91 Rugby Football League season|1990–91 season]]. The transfer fee was set by a tribunal, with Wigan paying an initial £65,000 to Rochdale, plus an additional £15,000 when Cowie made his international debut.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Macklin |first1=Keith |title=Officials looking for unity |url=https://archive.org/details/NewsUK1991UKEnglish/Oct%2009%201991%2C%20The%20Times%2C%20%2364146%2C%20UK%20%28en%29/page/n37/mode/2up |work=The Times |date=9 October 1991 |page=39 |location=London |via=[[Internet Archive]]}}</ref>

During the [[1991–92 Rugby Football League season|1991–92 season]], Cowie played for defending champions Wigan as a substitute in their [[1991 World Club Challenge]] victory against the visiting [[Penrith Panthers]].

During the [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season|1992–93 season]], Cowie was a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] (replacing [[Steve Hampson]]) in Wigan's 5–4 victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[1992 Lancashire Cup]] Final at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St Helens]] on 18 October 1992.<ref name="1992-1993 Lancashire Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2745|title=1992–1993 Lancashire Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180713170348/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2745|archive-date=13 July 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref> He also played in Wigan's 15–8 victory over [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]] in the [[1992–93 Regal Trophy]] Final at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on 23 January 1993,<ref name="23 January 1993: Bradford 8 Wigan 15 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=3004|title=23rd January 1993: Bradford 8 Wigan 15 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180619140552/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=3004|archive-date=19 June 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref>

During the [[1993–94 Rugby Football League season|1993–94 season]], Cowie played in the 2–33 defeat by [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in the [[1993–94 League Cup (rugby league)|1993–94 Regal Trophy]] Final at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on 22 January 1994. Cowie travelled with defending champions [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] to Brisbane, playing as a {{rlp|pr}} in their [[1994 World Club Challenge]] victory over Australian premiers, the [[Brisbane Broncos]].

During the [[1994–95 Rugby Football League season|1994–95 season]], Cowie played in the 40–10 victory over [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[1994–95 League Cup (rugby league)|1994–95 Regal Trophy]] Final at [[John Smith's Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on 28 January 1995.<ref name="28 January 1995: Warrington 10 Wigan 40 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2931|title=28th January 1995: Warrington 10 Wigan 40 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-date=14 July 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714151036/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2931|url-status=dead}}</ref>

During the [[1995–96 Rugby Football League season|1995–96 season]], Cowie played in the 25–16 victory over St Helens in the [[1995–96 League Cup (rugby league)|1995–96 Regal Trophy]] Final at Alfred McAlpine Stadium, Huddersfield on 13 January 1996.<ref name="13 January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|title=13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181007005311/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|archive-date=7 October 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Cowie played for Wigan Warriors from the bench in their [[1998 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos]].

In June 2000, Cowie converted a drop goal, the only one-pointer he scored in his career, in a 30–18 victory over the [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Hadfield|first=Dave|title=Rugby League: Cowie Stops Raging Bulls; Bradford Bulls 18 Wigan Warriors 30|url=http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P2-5093409.html|archive-url=https://archive.today/20131224131541/http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P2-5093409.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=24 December 2013|newspaper=The Independent|date=1 July 2000|location=London|page=25|url-access=subscription }}</ref> He played for Wigan at prop forward in their [[2000 Super League Grand Final]] loss against St Helens.

Cowie played for Wigan Warriors from the bench in their [[2001 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the Bradford Bulls. This turned out to be his last appearance for the club, as he announced his retirement shortly after the match.<ref>{{cite web|title=Cowie calls it a day|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/wales/1600575.stm|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=24 December 2013|date=15 October 2001}}</ref>

===Representative career===
Cowie was selected to go on the [[1992 Great Britain Lions tour of Australia and New Zealand]].

Cowie won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] while at Wigan in 1995 against England, and France, and in the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]] against Western Samoa, in 1996 against England, in 1998 against England, and in 1999 against Ireland, and Scotland, and won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] while at Wigan in 1993 against France, and in 1998 against New Zealand (2 matches).<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> Cowie was also selected for the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]], but withdrew from the squad due to injury.<ref>{{cite web |title=Wales lose vice-captain Cowie |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/world_cup_2000/group_2/975915.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=28 August 2022 |date=17 October 2000}}</ref>
